data.title: Next Generation Identity Verification Platform (NGIVP)
data.agency: Department of Justice, Federal Bureau of Investigation
data.placeOfPerformance: CJIS Complex, 1000 Custer Hollow Road, Clarksburg, WV 26306
data.summary: Full and open competition for the design, development, testing, deployment, and maintenance of a Next Generation Identity Verification Platform for the FBI CJIS Division. The system must support multi-modal biometric matching (fingerprint, facial recognition, iris, voice) with sub-second response times and 500,000+ daily transactions. Government Independent Cost Estimate is $47.5 million over 5 years. CPFF contract type with best value trade-off evaluation.

data.overview: Design, develop, test, deploy, and maintain a next-generation identity verification platform supporting multi-modal biometric matching for 18,000+ law enforcement agencies. Must process 500K+ transactions daily with sub-500ms response time.

requirements.description: Multi-modal biometric matching: fingerprint, facial recognition, iris, voice
NIST SP 800-76 and FBI EBTS compliance
FedRAMP High + CJIS Security Policy compliance

submissionCompliance.description: Submit via FBI CJIS eSolicitation Portal

incumbentInfo.notes: No incumbent contractor identified. This appears to be a new development effort for a next-generation system.

risks.description: Large-scale biometric system development requires specialized expertise not typically held by general IT consultancies
CJIS Security Policy compliance requires extensive background checks and security clearances
25 FTE minimum staffing requirement is substantial
CPFF contract type requires robust cost accounting system (DCAA-auditable)

data.narrativeSummary: Limited relevant past performance for biometric identity systems at this scale.
data.gapAnalysis: Significant gap in biometric system development experience. Would need teaming partner with biometric domain expertise.

criteria.name: TECHNICAL_FIT
criteria.rationale: System integration and cloud-native development capabilities are relevant, but biometric algorithm expertise is a gap requiring a teaming partner.

criteria.name: PAST_PERFORMANCE_RELEVANCE
criteria.rationale: No direct biometric system past performance. Some relevant large-scale identity/access management experience.

criteria.name: PRICING_POSITION
criteria.rationale: Government IGE of $47.5M provides clear pricing target. CPFF contract type is manageable.

criteria.name: STRATEGIC_ALIGNMENT
criteria.rationale: Large federal law enforcement IT modernization aligns with growth strategy.

criteria.name: INCUMBENT_RISK
criteria.rationale: No incumbent identified. New development effort levels the playing field.

data.summaryJustification: Large, strategically valuable FBI IT modernization with clear government estimate. Requires teaming for biometric expertise.
data.decisionRationale: CONDITIONAL_GO pending identification of biometric domain teaming partner and verification of CJIS compliance readiness.
data.confidenceExplanation: Moderate confidence — opportunity is real and well-defined, but requires teaming to be competitive.

data.strategy: Price to the government's IGE of $47.5M over 5 years. CPFF contract type allows cost recovery with fixed fee. Target 8-10% fee rate.
data.competitivePosition: Must price competitively against IGE while demonstrating technical superiority to justify best-value selection.
assumptions: Government IGE of $47.5M is the pricing benchmark
pricingRisks: Cost realism analysis may challenge low-ball pricing
data.basisOfEstimate: Government IGE: $47.5M over 5 years. Pricing based on 25 FTE team with blended rate modeling.
